_status: RETIRED — superseded by BL-010 (documentation truth pass, 2026-07-08). This file is no longer an active backlog. Its earlier `current_state` said 'No endpoint is functional yet', which is no longer true: the MVP is implemented and dogfood-deployed. The task list (T01–T19), milestones, dependency graph, and dispatch state have been removed to stop maintaining two divergent backlogs. The conventions block below is retained because it is still accurate and useful to agents.

_current_sources.reality_audit: docs/plans/engram-mvp-audit.md (historical snapshot — its findings are resolved by BL-001 through BL-010)

_historical_note: The T01–T19 task scheme below was the pre-MVP planning breakdown. All build tasks (T01–T18) were merged; old T19 (Proxmox deployment) was rescoped into BL-007 (deployment artifacts) and BL-009 (dogfood deployment), both complete. The MVP execution backlog is BL-001 through BL-010 (all complete); BL-011+ is post-MVP. See docs/plans/engram-mvp-backlog.md.

conventions.commit_style: conventional commits (feat:, fix:, test:, docs:, refactor:)
conventions.pr_per_task: one branch, one PR per task. Do not bundle unrelated changes.
conventions.rls_note: The application sets app.tenant_id and app.principal_id per DB session via SET LOCAL. The get_session dependency does this based on the authenticated principal.
conventions.check_constraints: All enum-like fields (kind, visibility, review_status, source_type, sensitivity, conflict_type) have CHECK constraints in the DB. Pydantic models use Literal types to validate before the DB does.
conventions.sensitivity_vocabulary: normal | sensitive | restricted. The older 'confidential' value was an enum-drift bug fixed by BL-001; it must never reappear.
conventions.dedup_scope: Dedup is (tenant_id, workspace_id, principal_id, content_hash) WHERE valid_to IS NULL AND review_status != 'rejected', with NULLS NOT DISTINCT (Postgres 15+) so NULL workspace_id items dedup correctly. Enforced by unique index idx_memitems_dedup.
conventions.append_first: Content is never UPDATEd. Metadata changes (wing, room, visibility, review_status) go through PATCH which writes to item_events audit table then updates the column. Content changes require supersession (new row + mark old).
conventions.security_invoker_views: active_memories and cca_ledger views use WITH (security_invoker = true) so RLS policies apply through the view. App code can query these views safely.
